problem_statement: AI-assisted (GPT/Codex) code generation in banking software introduces ethical and regulatory risks — IP infringement, bias, opacity, and over-reliance on automation — that lack measurable, real-time oversight aligned with AML/KYC/ECOA rules.
magnitude_evidence: Automation Dependency Score of 60% and Composite Ethical Risk Score of 22.60% (computed on synthetic data)
insufficiency_evidence: Academic approaches to ethical risk quantification remain largely qualitative or generic; existing quantitative models are too general or not aligned with banking rules
solution_mechanisms: human expert (compliance officer) rating of explainability
governance & feedback layer triggering retraining on threshold breach
notes: Findings based on synthetic data only (authors note limitation); OCR text is garbled in places and EG formula is duplicated from BPI.
